symptoms.symptom_name: Severe colicky lower abdominal pain in waves after a heavy rich meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-prandial exacerbation of colonic motility (gastrocolic reflex) fits a secretory/motility flare.
Wave-like colicky pain is a classic obstruction pattern — non-specific and cannot discriminate on its own.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Secretory watery diarrhoea is the cardinal feature of microscopic colitis.
Acute watery diarrhoea can be infective; afebrile status and known colitis make a flare more likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Passing abundant flatus and stool (no absolute constipation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued passage of stool and flatus argues against complete mechanical obstruction, but does not exclude partial/early obstruction.
Vomiting is expected with significant obstruction; its absence lowers the probability.
Afebrile status mildly argues against an inflammatory/infective surgical process; early appendicitis can be afebrile, and the reading is unverified.
Absence of fever lowers but does not exclude an infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood or mucus in stool

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe inflammatory bowel disease flare (Crohn / ulcerative colitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloody diarrhoea is characteristic of a severe IBD flare; non-bloody stool argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse lower-abdominal pain, not localised to right iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of RLQ localisation lowers appendicitis probability, though early appendicitis can present with diffuse periumbilical/central pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known histologically-confirmed microscopic (lymphocytic) colitis, prior colonoscopy macroscopically normal

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior tissue diagnosis strongly supports a flare as the cause of recurrent watery diarrhoea — but must not anchor away from a new surgical process.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe inflammatory bowel disease flare (Crohn / ulcerative colitis)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recent macroscopically normal colonoscopy argues against active severe IBD.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense health anxiety / fear of obstruction or rupture

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress amplifies symptom reporting but has no discriminating diagnostic value; it must not itself raise or lower the triage tier.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-distended abdomen with no guarding, rigidity or rebound; hyperactive bowel sounds; normal percussion

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft non-peritonitic abdomen with hyperactive sounds confirms a motility/secretory picture — but this is a sealed in-person finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
Absence of peritonism on examination is what excludes a surgical abdomen; because it requires palpation it is the pivotal telehealth limitation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Well-perfused, moist mucous membranes (adequate hydration on examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring hydration despite watery diarrhoea — a sealed clinician assessment; the AI can only infer hydration from patient-reported intake and symptoms.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Microscopic colitis flare with visceral colic
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Watery non-bloody diarrhoea with preserved stool/flatus passage, no vomiting, and a prior tissue diagnosis together point to a benign flare rather than a surgical abdomen.
The findings that actually exclude a surgical abdomen are physical-examination findings — unavailable to the AI Doctor, which is why in-person assessment is mandatory.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination (palpation for peritonism, auscultation, percussion)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step — it excludes a surgical abdomen and is the basis of the benign attribution; must be arranged same-day because it cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and C-reactive protein
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for an inflammatory/infective process (raised WCC/CRP) that would argue against a simple flare.
Characterises the diarrhoea, excludes infection, and quantifies mucosal inflammation to guide any budesonide decision.

Not indicated routinely — in the source, a clinical examination excluded a surgical abdomen and avoided an unnecessary CT; imaging is reserved for cases where examination raises obstruction/perforation concern or the patient deteriorates.
Already performed recently with a macroscopically normal result and a tissue diagnosis; no acute indication to repeat.
